title: RV8GR page and data page register timing
purpose: Prove U23 page-register edge timing beside the existing U32 data-page path so SETPG, SETDP, and jump targets stay understandable and testable.

chips.role: Page register for PC high byte during jump
OR gate that forms PG_CLK from /T2 and /PG_cond
Data page register used by load/store data addresses

behavior.pg_clk: PG_CLK=/T2 OR /PG_cond. During SETPG T2 it goes LOW, then the return HIGH at T2 end is the 74HC574 capture edge.
behavior.setpg: SETPG is MUX_SEL=1 and AC_WR=0; U23 captures IBUS on the PG_CLK rising edge.
behavior.setdp: DP_Load=T2 AND XOR_MODE AND /ADDR_MODE AND /AC_WR; U32 captures IBUS on DP_Load rising.
behavior.jump_target: PC load target is {PG,IRL}; SETPG $10 followed by J $00 targets $1000.
behavior.output_policy: PG and DP are each driven by one active 74HC574 output bank. The named PC_LOAD bus is a package boundary shorthand, not a second physical driver.
behavior.separation: Normal SETPG and SETDP are separated by XOR_MODE and /ADDR_MODE. Risk vectors still record invalid overlap behavior.

trigger_edges: U23 captures on PG_CLK rising edge
U32 captures on DP_Load rising edge
timing.setup_hold_boundary: IBUS must meet 74HC574 setup/hold around PG_CLK and DP_Load rising edges; physical R/C delay and destination deadband remain blocked by timing_margins.json evidence.
unsafe_states: Treating PG_CLK LOW level as the latch event
SETPG and SETDP overlap on an invalid opcode/control mix

guarantees: U23 holds while PG_CLK is LOW at T2 start
U23 captures only on the PG_CLK rising edge at T2 end
Jump target combines PG high byte with IRL low byte
Clock profiles cover manual push, random 100 pushes, 50 kHz, 1 MHz, 2 MHz, and 5 MHz functional simulation

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
